# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book teaches you how GPT-style language models actually work, from the very first step — turning text into integers — all the way to the final step — turning vectors back into words.

The code language is Python 3.
Not because it is the only possible choice, but because it is readable, widely available, and close enough to executable math when written with plain functions and data structures.
A function is a formula.
A list is a matrix row.
The code reads like the equations.

The diagrams are animated.
Every figure in this book is generated by a Manim script, living alongside the prose.
You can reproduce any animation by running the corresponding script in `src/manim/`.

By the end, you will have a complete working microGPT — a minimal but correct GPT implementation in Python that you can run and inspect.
Not a toy metaphor: actual matrix multiplications, actual softmax, actual multi-head attention.
